raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "exhortation urging persistent action and temporary self-criticism in light of impending mortality",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "human life and opportunities for self-respect are finite",
    "self-abuse or contempt can serve as a temporary motivational tool",
    "duty or action should continue despite internal self-judgment",
    "awareness of death can reframe present self-treatment"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent within a Stoic framework that links mortality awareness to disciplined self-regulation, though the imperative to 'abuse' oneself risks surface contradiction with eventual self-respect",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "recipients accept finite lifespan as a motivator, interpret self-contempt as transient discipline rather than harm, and apply the reminder to sustain purposeful action",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is read as endorsing ongoing self-harm, nihilism about self-worth, or literal contempt without the framing of mortality as a spur to limited-time self-respect"
}
